steps.title: Identify the controlling idea
steps.content: The narrator opens by calling himself humble and absurd, but pivots: "I did come to know a truly great man." Everything that follows — "I spent years at his side. I watched the way he worked. I witnessed" — dwells on how much time the narrator spent admiring this person. That time and admiration are the passage’s central point.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘A person was fortunate enough to have found love despite his obvious shortcomings.’: Introduces romantic love, which the text never mentions; the relationship described is admiration, not romance. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘An exceptionally talented person professes humility to avoid intimidating others.’: The narrator calls himself humble, not exceptionally talented, and nothing suggests the great man feigns humility to manage others. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘A person appreciates the extensive time he passed in the company of someone he deeply admired.’: Matches the narrator's emphasis on years spent at the great man's side and his evident admiration. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘A truly brilliant person lets ideas mature in his mind before expressing them aloud.’: Shifts focus to how the great man develops ideas, but the passage is about the narrator's experience of watching him, not about the great man's creative process as the main idea. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
